title: Add Wallet page with balance, Add Money/Withdraw buttons, and Transaction History

requirements.text: Add a '/wallet' route to the existing TanStack Router configuration in App.tsx, rendering a new WalletPage component. The Header must remain visible on the Wallet page (shared RootLayout). Add a navigation link or icon in the Header to access the Wallet page.

quotes: Create a 'Wallet' page for Elite Gaming Hub

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to '/wallet' renders the WalletPage without replacing the Header.
A link or icon in the Header navigates to '/wallet'.
The existing '/' and '/registration' routes continue to work.

requirements.text: Create a WalletPage component at 'frontend/src/pages/WalletPage.tsx'. At the top of the page, display an 'Available Balance' card prominently showing a balance value (e.g., '₹0.00' as initial state). Below the balance card, render two action buttons side by side: 'Add Money' and 'Withdraw'. Below the buttons, render a 'Transaction History' section heading and a list of transactions. When no transactions exist, show an empty-state message such as 'No transactions yet'. All styling must follow the existing dark neon-red gaming theme (dark card background, neon red accents, Orbitron font for headings, Rajdhani for body text, neon glow on buttons).

quotes: It should show 'Available Balance' at the top.
show a 'Transaction History' list below the buttons

acceptanceCriteria: The page displays an 'Available Balance' label with a monetary value at the top.
'Add Money' and 'Withdraw' buttons are rendered below the balance card.
Both buttons use neon-red accent styling with neon glow, consistent with the app theme.
A 'Transaction History' section is visible below the buttons.
When the transaction list is empty, an empty-state message is displayed.
Orbitron font is used for section headings; Rajdhani for body/list text.
Dark card background and neon-red border/glow are applied consistently.

requirements.text: Add a Wallet data model to the Motoko backend in 'backend/main.mo'. Define a Transaction record type with fields: id (Nat), transactionType (Text — 'credit' or 'debit'), amount (Nat), description (Text), and timestamp (Int). Expose two query/update functions: (1) getWalletBalance() returning the current balance as a Nat, and (2) getTransactions() returning an array of Transaction records. Initialize the balance at 0 and the transaction list as empty.
acceptanceCriteria: getWalletBalance() query returns a Nat representing the current balance (initially 0).
getTransactions() query returns an array of Transaction records (initially empty).
Transaction type includes id, transactionType, amount, description, and timestamp fields.

requirements.text: Create a React Query hook 'useWallet' in 'frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts' (or a new file 'frontend/src/hooks/useWallet.ts') that fetches wallet balance via getWalletBalance() and transaction history via getTransactions() from the backend actor. Wire the WalletPage to use this hook: display the fetched balance in the Available Balance card and render the transaction list from the fetched data. Show a loading skeleton while data is fetching and an empty-state message when the transaction list is empty.
acceptanceCriteria: WalletPage fetches balance and transactions from the backend using React Query.
A loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched.
The Available Balance card reflects the value returned by getWalletBalance().
The Transaction History list renders items returned by getTransactions().
When getTransactions() returns an empty array, an empty-state message is shown.

constraints: All new frontend components must follow the existing dark neon-red gaming theme (Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts, neon red accents, dark card backgrounds, neon glow effects).
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor file backend/main.mo.
Do not add any external payment gateway integrations.
nonGoals: Implementing actual Add Money or Withdraw payment flows (buttons may be present but functional payment processing is out of scope).
User authentication-gated wallet (wallet is accessible anonymously for now).
Modifying existing Home or Registration page functionality.
